typography.notes: Use 'Press Start 2P' from Google Fonts for authentic retro 8-bit styling across all HUD and menu elements.

layout.game_container: max-w-4xl mx-auto border-4 border-white shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(0,232,216,0.5)] rounded-lg relative overflow-hidden bg-background
layout.hud: absolute top-0 left-0 w-full p-6 flex justify-between items-start text-white text-xl z-10 font-['Press_Start_2P'] drop-shadow-[2px_2px_0px_#000]

visual_effects.crt_scanlines: MANDATORY: Overlay the canvas with a scanline effect. Add a pointer-events-none absolute div over the canvas. Use CSS background: linear-gradient(rgba(18,16,16,0) 50%, rgba(0,0,0,0.25) 50%) and background-size: 100% 4px.
visual_effects.pixelation: Set ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = false on the canvas context so any drawn images or rects stay sharp and blocky.
visual_effects.text_shadow: All UI text over the canvas must have a sharp 2px 2px 0px #000 black shadow to pop against the blue water background.

components.StartScreen: Absolute positioned overlay, fully covering the canvas. Dark semi-transparent background (bg-black/80). Large game title logo text (text-primary with yellow text-shadow). Blinking 'PRESS SPACE TO START' text.
components.GameOverScreen: Absolute overlay, red/black theme. Large 'GAME OVER' text. Display final score and a 'PRESS R TO RESTART' button.
components.HUD: Top bar displaying: SCORE, LIVES (rendered as mini diver icons or hearts), COINS (yellow circle + number), LEVEL.

game_mechanics_and_canvas_instructions.engine: Pure HTML5 Canvas + React for UI overlays. Use a standard game loop with requestAnimationFrame inside a useEffect.
game_mechanics_and_canvas_instructions.physics: Underwater float/drift mechanics. Apply a gentle downward gravity. When the user presses UP/SPACE, apply an upward velocity (swim pulse). Horizontal movement (LEFT/RIGHT) should have slight friction/inertia to feel 'watery'.

entities.diver_hero: Draw a small blocky pixelated diver using fillRect clusters. Colors: hero_diver (#F8D870) and white. Hitbox should be slightly smaller than the visual.
entities.enemies: Bloopers (move in diagonal upward pulses, then drift down). Cheep-Cheeps (swim straight horizontally). Urchins (static or slow moving up/down obstacles).
entities.collectibles: Coins (yellow pulsing squares/circles). Power-ups: Mushroom equivalent (makes diver larger, +1 health), Star equivalent (temporary invincibility, rainbow flashing colors).
entities.environment: Draw simple seaweed stalks at the bottom using success color (#00A800) and some rising bubbles using accent color (#00E8D8) for parallax effect.

game_mechanics_and_canvas_instructions.level_progression: Arcade mode style. As score/time increases, spawn more enemies faster. Track 'Level' linearly based on time survived.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup a React application with Tailwind CSS and the 'Press Start 2P' font loaded from Google Fonts.
2. Do not use external game engines like Phaser; build the game loop in React using a single <canvas> element and requestAnimationFrame.

3. Keep all game state (player pos, enemies, coins) in a mutable useRef object rather than React state to avoid re-render performance issues. Only use React state for high-level UI like screens (Start, Playing, Game Over) and Score/Lives updates.
4. Implement the custom CSS for the CRT scanlines. Put it in your global CSS and apply the class to an overlay div.
5. Render the sprites programmatically using ctx.fillRect with an 8-bit scale (e.g., 1 game pixel = 4x4 canvas pixels) or use base64 pixel strings.
6. Do not skip adding `data-testid` to the UI overlays (buttons, screens).
